response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a thesis project, far short of the 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ required. Database experience centers on MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and there is no fixed-income or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital is retail-investment fintech, not investment banking. The gap between their web/full-stack specialization and this specialized quant-finance C++ role is too wide to justify an interview.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, embedded, and low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Proven low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec, async I/O)
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, none recent (last used 2015)
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary databases are MongoDB and MySQL

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD skills
